A standout shoyu ramen spot in Tokyo. Their signature menu is the “Special Shoyu Ramen.” Built on a chicken-based broth blended with multiple soy sauces, the first sip brings out the richness of chicken oil, deep umami from the soy sauce, and a subtle sweetness. As you continue, the flavors finish clean and balanced. The charcoal-grilled chashu adds a gentle smoky aroma that naturally melts into the broth✨ The straight noodles hold the soup well, delivering its flavor all the way through each bite. The chashu comes in a variety of cuts, each prepared differently—from rich, fatty pieces to lighter, more delicate textures—letting you enjoy a contrast of juiciness and mouthfeel. [Ramen Sakurai 麺屋 さくら井] - Location: 2 Chome-15-27 Kamirenjaku, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan - Hours: Mon & Sat 11:30 - 15:00 / 18:00 - 20:30, Tue/Thu/Fri 11:30 - 15:00, Sun 11:30 - 16:00 (Closed Wed) - Menu: Special Shoyu Ramen (¥2,000) #ramen #tokyoramen #shoyuramen #라멘

Creamy Cheesy Buldak Ramen 🍜🧀🔥 Ingredients 1 pack Buldak ramen 1 slice baby Swiss cheese 1 slice American cheese 1 slice Monterey Jack cheese 2 tbsp mayo Buldak sauce packet Buldak powder packet Chili oil 2 cloves garlic, minced 2 tbsp chopped green onions ½ cup heavy cream or milk Cooked ramen noodles Toppings Fried egg Mini sausages Dumplings Extra green onions Extra chili oil Instructions Cook the Buldak noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside. In a pan over low heat, add the baby Swiss, American, and Monterey Jack cheese slices. Add the mayo, Buldak sauce, Buldak powder, chili oil, minced garlic, and green onions on top of the cheese. Mix everything together until the cheese melts into a thick spicy paste. Pour in the heavy cream or milk and stir until smooth and creamy. Add the cooked noodles and toss until fully coated in the sauce. Top with fried egg, dumplings, mini sausages, extra green onions, and more chili oil if desired. Enjoy hot and creamy  #buldak #buldakramen #eat #food  #Foodie

Most people think they’re training hard. They’re not. They’re wasting energy before the real work even starts. This is the mistake 👇 People slowly climb weight after weight… doing half-effort sets the entire time. By the time they finally reach their working set? The nervous system is already fatigued. That means: 👉 Less output 👉 Less tension 👉 Less growth stimulus This is why feeder sets matter 👇 A feeder set is NOT a working set. Its job is to: • Prime the joints • Increase blood flow • Recruit muscle fibres • Wake up the nervous system Without creating unnecessary fatigue. Then when the real working set starts… Everything is ready. Maximum tension. Maximum force production. Maximum stimulus. And here’s the key 👇 If your working sets are truly hard enough… Performance should DROP after them. Less reps. Less output. Sometimes less weight. That’s fatigue accumulating in the muscle. That’s the signal for growth. If you can suddenly increase weight or reps after a “hard” set… It probably wasn’t hard enough. Train smarter. Not just longer. #WOOJIN — POV: Woojin as your ex-boyfriend who found happiness in you. It’s 2 a.m., and you can’t sleep, so you decide to scroll through your phone. Then, you come across a poster: “CONGRATULATIONS 4SHOBOIZ ON WINNING ROOKIE OF THE YEAR.” Your eyes fix on a black-haired guy with a slit eyebrow. Your heart beats faster than before. The feelings are familiar, just like when you first saw him on the hallway. But this time, something is different. His expression is cold. Happiness is no longer written on his face. He’s no longer the sweet, carefree boy you used to know. You sigh. 2 years ago “I think we should break up,” you said. He smiled softly. “You know you can’t joke about things like that. It’s not funny.” You turned your head and saw him focused on his laptop, headphones on, nodding along to the new beat he made without even looking at you. The two of you were in his studio. “Woojin, I’m serious. You’re about to debut, and a lot of people will be watching you. I don’t want our relationship to become a reason for people to hate you. You know how toxic this industry is, right?” Woojin finally turned toward you, his eyes locked onto yours. “I would give up everything just to be with you. I don’t care how toxic people are. I don’t care what everyone thinks of me. You are my only happiness. You are my dream. How am I supposed to find happiness when the person who gave meaning to my life is no longer beside me? It’s hard for me to breathe, to smile, to even dream without you here. Don’t ever think about leaving me. Please…” He said softly while holding your hands, his eyes tearing up as his voice almost broke. “I’m sorry. I need to see you on stage. I know you’ve been dreaming about it since you were five. Sometimes love is about letting go, and I hope you can let me go too. Do it, Woojin. Get that ROTY and SOTY you’ve always dreamed of. I’ll always be here supporting you no matter what.” You slowly let go of his hands. “And for the last time, as your lover... I love you, Woojin. And I always will.” You walked out of his studio, leaving him behind in tears.  #lngshot #kpop #edits #pov

Ultra Creamy Buldak Ramen 🍝🥟 Ingredients 1 pack Buldak ramen 1–2 tbsp butter 2 cloves garlic (minced) 1 tbsp Kewpie mayo Buldak sauce packet Buldak powder packet 1 tbsp chili oil ¾–1 cup heavy cream Mozzarella cheese Dumplings Sausages Green onions Sesame seeds Extra chili oil (for topping) Instructions Cook noodles until al dente, then drain and set aside. In a pan, melt butter over medium heat. Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant. Add Kewpie mayo, Buldak sauce, and powder packet. Mix well until it turns thick and glossy. Add chili oil and stir. Pour in heavy cream and mix until smooth, creamy, and slightly thickened (that rich orange color 🔥). Add noodles and toss until fully coated. Sprinkle mozzarella cheese on top and let it melt into the sauce. Add dumplings and sausages on top. Cover and let everything heat through. Finish with green onions, sesame seeds, and extra chili oil drizzle. . #buldak #buldakramen #eat #cook #Foodie
